paragraph_content.content: Can be applied for ya
The composition of air is often given in terms of only two principal species in the gas mixture
The molecular weight of oxygen is 0.032 kg/mol and of nitrogen is 0.028 kg/mol. Condition: 25 oC and 101300 Pa abs.

item_content.content: i. Mean molecular weight of the air
ii. Mass fraction of both oxygen and nitrogen
iii. Molar concentration of both oxygen and nitrogen

paragraph_content.content: • In multicomponent mixture, each species will normally move at different velocities
Defined in term of mass densities
Defined in term of molar concentration
Velocity of a species in relative to average velocity
Diffusion velocity of species i relative to
v _ { i d = } v _ { i } - \mathrm { v }
mass-average velocity
Diffusion velocity of species i relative to
V _ { i d = } v _ { i } - \mathsf { V }
molar-average velocity
A gas mixture containing 65 mol% NH3, 8 mol% N2, 24 mol% H2 and 3 mol% Ar is flowing through a pipe 25 mm in diameter at a total pressure of 4 atm.
The velocities of the components are as follows:
Calculate the molar-average, mass-average velocity, and mass diffusion velocity of H2.

item_content.content: • In 1855, Fick quantified the observation of diffusion
• Flux is the amount of the particular species passes through a cross section area at a given time (can be in mass or molar unit)
• Fick’s first law proposed molar flux for isothermal, isobaric system

paragraph_content.content: • For diffusion of a binary mixture of A and B in z-direction

content.math_content: J _ {A, z} = - D _ {A B} \frac {d c _ {A}}{d z}

paragraph_content.content: ????,?? = molar flux of component A (mol/m2.s)
?????? = diffusivity or diffusion coefficient (m2/s)
?????? ???? = concentration gradient (
\mathtt { C _ { A } }
in mol/m3, and z in m)
Can be expressed in another form

content.math_content: J _ {A, z} = - c D _ {A B} \frac {d y _ {A}}{d z}

paragraph_content.content: where cA = cyA

content.math_content: j _ {A, z} = \mathrm {m a s s f l u x o f A (k g / m ^ {2} . s)}

paragraph_content.content: A mixture of He and N2 gas is contained in a pipe at 298 K and 1 atm total pressure. At one end of the pipe the partial pressure of He is 0.6 atm and the partial pressure of He is 0.2 atm at the other end (0.2m apart). Calculate the molar and mass flux of He at steady state if DHe-N2 is 0.687 x 10-4 m2/s.

A plate of iron is exposed to a carburizing (carbon-rich) atmosphere on one side and a decarburizing (carbon-deficient) atmosphere on the other side at 700 oC. If a condition of steady state is achieved, calculate the mass diffusion flux of carbon through the plate if the concentration of carbon at position of 5 and 10 mm beneath the carburizing surface are 100 and 66.67 mol/m3, respectively. Assume a diffusion coefficient of 3 x 10-7 cm2/s at this temperature.
Flux is contributed by:
• For binary system with constant average velocity, molar diffusion flux can be expressed as

content.math_content: J _ {A, z} = c _ {A} \left(V _ {A d, z}\right) = c _ {A} \left(v _ {A, z} - V _ {z}\right)

paragraph_content.content: ??????,?? is the molar diffusion velocity
v _ { A , z }
is the velocity of component A
???? is the molar-average velocity of stream

item_content.content: Diffusion velocity is measured relative to the moving fluid
To a stationary observer, A is moving faster than the bulk of the phase since its diffusion velocity
is added to that of the bulk phase (V)

paragraph_content.content: Multiply with cA

content.math_content: c _ {A} v _ {A} = c _ {A} V _ {A d} + c _ {A} \mathsf {V}

paragraph_content.content: Diffusion flux of A relative to moving fluid (concentration gradient contribution)

content.math_content: c _ {A} v _ {A} = c _ {A} V _ {A d} + c _ {A} \mathsf {V}

paragraph_content.content: Total flux of A relative to stationary point
Convective flux of A relative to stationary point (bulk motion contribution)
Let NA = total flux = ????????
Introduce new term total flux represented by NA

content.math_content: J _ {A, z} = c _ {A} \left(v _ {A, z} - V _ {z}\right) = - c D _ {A B} \frac {d y _ {A}}{d z}

paragraph_content.content: Rearrangement,

content.math_content: c _ {A} v _ {A, z} = - c D _ {A B} \frac {d y _ {A}}{d z} + c _ {A} V _ {z}

paragraph_content.content: Molar-average velocity,

content.math_content: V _ {z} = \frac {1}{c} \big (c _ {A} v _ {A, z} + c _ {B} v _ {B, z} \big)
c _ {A} V _ {z} = y _ {A} \big (c _ {A} v _ {A, z} + c _ {B} v _ {B, z} \big)
c _ {A} v _ {A, z} = - c D _ {A B} \frac {d y _ {A}}{d z} + y _ {A} \big (c _ {A} v _ {A, z} + c _ {B} v _ {B, z} \big)
N _ {A, z} = - c D _ {A B} \frac {d y _ {A}}{d z} + y _ {A} \big (N _ {A, z} + N _ {B, z} \big)
